title: poikilospermum singalense
intro: Poikilospermum singalense, also known as the Ceylon wood fern, is a type of flowering plant native to Sri Lanka, characterized by its distinctive fern-like foliage. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Poikilospermum singalense is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal remedies, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
